name: Credit Sesame
description_short: Free credit monitoring with TransUnion score, personalized loan and credit card recommendations, identity theft protection, and $50K-$1M identity theft insurance.
description_long:
Credit Sesame is a Mountain View, California-based fintech founded in 2010 that provides free credit monitoring and personalized financial product recommendations. The company serves as a gateway for consumers to understand their credit profile and find matching financial products.

Free features include:
- TransUnion VantageScore 3.0 credit score (updated monthly)
- Credit monitoring with alerts for changes
- $50,000 identity theft insurance
- Personalized credit card, loan, and savings recommendations
- Credit report card showing factors affecting your score
- Credit score simulator ("What if I pay off this card?")

Premium plans ($9.95-$24.95/month) add:
- Scores from all 3 bureaus
- Daily score updates
- Full credit reports
- Up to $1 million identity theft insurance
- Dark web monitoring
- SSN alerts
- Court records monitoring

Credit Sesame makes money primarily through referral fees when users apply for recommended financial products (credit cards, loans, etc.). This means the free tier is genuinely free — you're the product in the sense that they recommend products, but there's no bait-and-switch.

The platform is particularly useful for people rebuilding credit who want to track their progress and find credit products they can actually qualify for. Credit Sesame's matching algorithm considers your actual credit profile, so the cards and loans they recommend have a realistic approval chance.

Credit Sesame has over 15 million members and is a TransUnion partner. Consumers tracking their progress may eventually qualify for better terms on installment loans and other financial products as their scores improve.
pros: Genuinely free tier with useful features
Product recommendations based on your actual credit profile
Credit score simulator helps plan improvements
No credit card required for free tier
cons: Free tier only shows TransUnion score (1 of 3 bureaus)
Monthly updates only (not daily) on free tier
Revenue model means product recommendations are ads
VantageScore 3.0 (not FICO, which most lenders use)
Can feel pushy with product recommendations
